_meta.purpose: Erdős problem registry for AIIA autonomous problem loops.
_meta.source_of_truth: https://www.erdosproblems.com — the loop re-verifies status against it + the literature on every run. Do NOT trust the `status` field here as ground truth; it is a training-knowledge prior with a confidence tag, and VERIFYING it is the loop's first job.

modes.literature: Find whether it's already settled / what the current frontier paper is. Cheapest, highest hit-rate, runs first on every problem.
modes.computational: Counterexample search, small-case enumeration, statistic-gathering that can confirm/refute or reshape the conjecture. Verifiable by re-execution.
modes.proof_search: Attempt a novel argument, lemma, reduction, or special case. High value, high hallucination risk — gated hardest.
modes.formalization: Encode a known or newly-found result in Lean. A machine-checked proof cannot be a hallucination — the strongest verification we have.
modes.generalization: Propose new conjectures (analogues, strengthenings) backed by computational evidence. New territory: generating problems, not only attacking them.

_meta.computational_surface: 0..1 — how much a brute-force / enumeration probe can move the needle. Picker favors high values for early runs (safe, demoable wins).
_meta.loop_first_move: What the loop should attempt first; never start at proof_search on an open hard problem.

problems.name: Erdős–Straus conjecture
problems.statement: For every n ≥ 2, 4/n = 1/a + 1/b + 1/c has a solution in positive integers.
problems.loop_first_move: Enumerate solutions to large n, characterize the residue classes where n is hard (the surviving cases mod 840), gather statistics on solution multiplicity. Pure verifiable computation — ideal first demo.

problems.name: Sierpiński / Erdős 5/n analogue
problems.statement: 5/n = 1/a + 1/b + 1/c solvable for all n ≥ 2 (analogue of Erdős–Straus).
problems.loop_first_move: Same enumeration harness as Erdős–Straus; cross-problem reuse is the point — proves the technique-transfer hypothesis.

problems.name: Erdős–Turán conjecture on additive bases
problems.statement: If A ⊆ ℕ is an additive basis of order 2, then the representation function r(n) = #{(a,b): a+b=n, a,b∈A} is unbounded.
problems.loop_first_move: Literature frontier survey only. Low computational surface; this is a long-chain proof problem — a Fable-class deductive target, not a brute-force one. Do not let the loop emit a 'proof' here without the full verification ladder.

problems.name: Erdős conjecture on arithmetic progressions
problems.statement: If A ⊆ ℕ and Σ_{a∈A} 1/a diverges, then A contains arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions.
problems.loop_first_move: Map the frontier (Green–Tao gives primes; the general conjecture is wide open). Synthesis target: what reductions exist? No proof claims without Lean.

problems.name: Erdős–Gyárfás / covering by {2^a·3^b}
problems.statement: Questions on covering integers by numbers of the form 2^a·3^b and related density problems.
problems.loop_first_move: Verify exact statement + status first (status_confidence low). Then computational density gathering.

problems.name: Erdős minimum modulus problem (covering congruences)
problems.statement: Is there a bound on the minimum modulus of a covering system with distinct moduli? (Resolved: Hough 2015 — no covering system with min modulus > 10^16; later work tightened.)
problems.loop_first_move: Study/Lean-formalize the Hough distortion-method core. A 'solved' entry kept deliberately as a formalization + technique-extraction target.

problems.name: Erdős discrepancy problem
problems.statement: For any ±1 sequence, sums over homogeneous APs (d, 2d, 3d, …) are unbounded. (Resolved: Tao 2015.)
problems.loop_first_move: Technique-extraction: Tao's GPY/entropy decomposition. Generalization mode: what analogues remain open? SAT-solver lower bounds (Konev–Lisitsa) are a verifiable computational re-run.

problems.name: Erdős–Faber–Lovász conjecture
problems.statement: The union of n edge-disjoint copies of K_n is n-edge-colorable. (Resolved for large n: Kang–Kelly–Kühn–Methuku–Osthus 2021.)
problems.loop_first_move: Verify small-n cases computationally; study the absorption method. Open remnant: explicit small-n bounds — a verifiable computational frontier.

problems.name: Erdős distinct distances problem
problems.statement: n points in the plane determine ≳ n/√(log n) distinct distances. (Essentially resolved: Guth–Katz 2015, up to the log factor.)
problems.loop_first_move: Frontier: the remaining √(log n) gap. Generalization: higher dimensions, where it is still open. Technique-extraction of polynomial method.

problems.name: Erdős unit distance problem
problems.statement: n points in the plane determine O(n^{1+ε}) unit distances? (Open; best known ~n^{4/3}.)
problems.loop_first_move: Computational: search configurations maximizing unit distances for small n (known optimal configs are a verifiable target), compare to the n^{4/3} bound.

problems.name: Erdős–Szekeres (happy ending) — convex polygons
problems.statement: ES(n) = 2^{n-2}+1 points in general position force a convex n-gon. (Suk 2017 proved 2^{n+o(n)}; exact conjecture open.)
problems.loop_first_move: Verify exact small values (ES(6)=17 was a 2006 computer proof); the empty-hexagon and exact-ES frontiers are verifiable enumeration targets.

problems.name: Erdős–Hajnal conjecture
problems.statement: For every graph H there is c(H)>0 such that H-free graphs on n vertices contain a clique or independent set of size n^{c(H)}.
problems.loop_first_move: Map which H are resolved (recent progress on specific H). Computational: verify c(H) lower bounds for small H. Synthesis target — many sub-cases share structure.

problems.name: Erdős–Rado sunflower conjecture
problems.statement: A k-uniform family with > c^k sets contains a sunflower with 3 petals. (Major progress: Alweiss–Lovett–Wu–Zhang 2019/2021, c^k log... — exact c open.)
problems.loop_first_move: Frontier survey of the ALWZ bound + successors; the gap to the conjectured c^k is the target. Formalization candidate (the ALWZ argument is relatively short).

problems.name: Erdős multiplication table problem
problems.statement: How many distinct products appear in the n×n multiplication table? (Order of magnitude resolved: Ford 2008 — n^2/(log n)^{δ}(log log n)^{3/2}.)
problems.loop_first_move: Directly computable: count distinct products for growing n, fit against Ford's order of magnitude. Clean verifiable demo with a known answer to check against.

problems.name: Erdős minimum overlap problem
problems.statement: Split {1,…,2n} into two equal sets A,B; minimize the max overlap of A with translates of B. Determine the limiting constant.
problems.loop_first_move: Optimization search for the constant at growing n; tighten the known numerical bounds. High computational surface, underexplored — a strong candidate for a genuine numerical contribution.
